title: AI arbiter can be selected even when no arbiter address is deployed

evidence.quote: <GlowButton type="submit" variant="primary" loading={submitting} className="w-full py-3 text-base">

reasoning: The UI explicitly handles the case where getArbiterAddress(chainId) is missing by showing an aiArbiterNotDeployed warning, but the deploy button remains available and handleSubmit still passes getArbiterAddress(currentChainId) as the arbiter when useAIArbiter is true. That turns a known unavailable configuration into a failed wallet/contract submission instead of blocking early with a clear validation error.
reproduction: Use a chain where getArbiterAddress(chainId) is falsy, select AI arbiter, and press Deploy. The form is still submittable even though the page already knows the AI arbiter is not deployed.
recommendation: Disable AI arbiter selection or form submission when no arbiter address exists for the active chain, and surface a validation toast before calling writeContractAsync.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests cover chain-specific arbiter availability or submit-button gating.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock getArbiterAddress to return null/undefined for the active chain, select AI arbiter, and assert submit is blocked before writeContractAsync is called.
minimumFixScope: Add AI-arbiter availability validation and button gating in frontend/src/app/[locale]/create/page.tsx.
